message:
feat(fm-wasm): add WebRenderer selection with canvas2d/WebGPU fallback and lens edit bindings

Two additions to the wasm crate, both user-facing.

1) WebRenderer selection. A new `WebRendererKind` enum (`canvas2d`, `webgpu`)
   with camelCase serde names lets callers request a renderer at render
   time. `ResolvedRenderer` records what was requested, what was actually
   used, and an optional `fallback_reason: Option<&'static str>` when the
   two differ — for example a `webgpu` request on a browser that does not
   expose `navigator.gpu`. `RendererRuntimeSummary` is the wasm-bindgen
   friendly shape returned to JS: `{ requested, actual, fallbackReason? }`.
   The summary skips `fallback_reason` entirely when there was no fallback,
   so successful runs do not advertise a nonexistent reason field.

   The wasm32 cfg gate is intentional — the fallback path only exists in
   the browser build, but the enum itself and a small `#[cfg(test)]` shim
   for `ResolvedRenderer` are compiled natively so unit tests can exercise
   the selection logic without a browser.

2) Lens editing. Pulls in `apply_parse_lens_edit` and `build_parse_lens` from
   `fm_parser` (newly re-exported there) and wires them into the Diagram
   surface so JS callers can build a lens against a parsed source map and
   apply a single-range text edit without reparsing from scratch. This is
   the piece that makes incremental authoring tools viable on top of the
   fm-parser `MermaidFormatComplement` work.

No behavior change for existing canvas2d renders: `WebRendererKind::default()`
is `Canvas2d`, which is what callers were implicitly getting before.
